p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; stable orientation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent turgidity throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant leaf drop or wilting observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy; foliage remains vibrant and upright, indicating effective moisture retention.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot; two primary leaves with smaller central growth; shares space with p4.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The central sprout has remained static; leaf margins show persistent dehydration compared to the baseline.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Stressed; leaf margin necrosis persists, confirming the A5 sensor failure noted in previous reports.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot; two leaves; white rabbit anchor (5cm) present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf tip necrosis on the larger leaf has stabilized; no further progression of tissue death since T-4.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; the plant is holding its current state despite VPD stress.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen; located near the rim of the black pot shared with p2.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains in a dormant/slow-growth state; no significant morphological changes observed over the 5-day period.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; no signs of active decay or rapid growth.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white granular material (starch water residue) is consistent across all pots, confirming user care.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or secondary seedlings detected.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: None; soil texture appears consistent with recent hydration.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Initial application of starch water observed; white residue appears on soil surface.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-1: Stabilization phase; no significant changes in plant posture or soil moisture appearance.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Maintenance phase; no new stress indicators or growth spurts.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The garden is in a state of 'managed stability'. The starch water application has been successfully integrated into the soil matrix without causing adverse reactions. p2 remains the primary concern due to persistent margin dehydration, while p1 and p3 show resilience.
vision_report.anomalies: None; the white granular material on the soil is identified as the starch water residue from the 2026-04-09 action.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently stable. The human intervention (starch water) has been successfully applied, evidenced by the uniform white residue across the soil surfaces. p1 continues to be the most robust specimen. p2 requires continued manual monitoring as the A5 sensor remains unreliable. No new physiological stressors have emerged in the last 24 hours.
